response.justification: Szabo brings strong senior-level systems experience and direct geospatial domain knowledge through GISCollective and Green Map projects, which are relevant to routing infrastructure. However, his C++ expertise is outdated (last significant work in 2014–2015), and his recent career has pivoted heavily toward TypeScript/Node.js full-stack development. While he demonstrates the ability to learn new tech stacks and has worked at scale with distributed systems, returning to modern systems-level C++ programming would require demonstrated capability before he could contribute effectively to a routing pipeline.
strengths: 15+ years of senior-level development with proven track record learning diverse tech stacks across web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems.
Direct geospatial domain expertise through founding GISCollective (geo-data platform) and contributing to Green Map's navigation and mapping systems.
Substantial experience optimizing high-throughput, low-latency systems at scale: handled millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby using async I/O, caching, and real-time data processing.
concerns: Last significant C++ work was 11 years ago (National Instruments, 2014–2015); recent career focuses on TypeScript, Node.js, and web-stack technologies, creating a substantial skills gap for a modern C++-heavy role.
Geospatial experience is in data collection and GIS platforms, not routing optimization, tile-based systems, or navigation-specific algorithms required for production routing pipelines.
Currently in a career gap (caregiving, October 2025–present) after full-stack web roles; no recent systems-level or performance-critical programming to demonstrate readiness for low-level C++ work.
